level 19 .byte $0d, $0f, $0f, $0c, $02, $0b, $02, $10 .byte $02, $0b, $02, $10, $02, $02, $03, $03 .byte $04, $09, $05, $02, $07, $08, $07, $08 .byte $01, $01, $02, $02, $0d, $0f, $0f, $0c ; level 20 .byte $02, $10, $07, $05, $02, $02, $10, $0d .byte $0f, $0f, $0c, $07, $0e, $08, $07, $0e .byte $08, $02, $02, $03, $03, $03, $07, $05 .byte $02, $02, $02, $03, $03, $02, $02, $0d ; level 21 .byte $0f, $0c, $07, $08, $07, $08, $07, $0e .byte $0e, $08, $0d, $0f, $0f, $0c, $02, $0b .byte $02, $10, $02, $0b, $02, $10, $07, $08 .byte $03, $03, $03, $04, $09, $09, $05, $02 ; level 22 .byte $02, $0b, $02, $10, $0b, $07, $08, $07 .byte $0e, $08, $07, $0e, $08, $03, $03, $04 .byte $05, $03, $02, $02, $02, $01, $01, $01 .byte $02, $02, $03, $03, $03, $02, $0d, $0c ; level 23 .byte $0d, $0f, $0f, $0c, $02, $02, $07, $08 .byte $07, $08, $02, $03, $04, $05, $02, $03 .byte $03, $02, $02, $07, $0e, $08, $07, $08 .byte $02, $02, $03, $03, $02, $02, $07, $0e ; level 24 .byte $08, $07, $0e, $08, $07, $0e, $08, $02 .byte $0b, $02, $0b, $02, $10, $02, $10, $07 .byte $08, $07, $08, $02, $02, $03, $03, $02 .byte $02, $02, $03, $04, $05, $0c, $02, $02 ; level 25 .byte $03, $04, $05, $0c, $02, $02, $0b, $02 .byte $10, $02, $0b, $07, $08, $07, $0e, $0e .byte $0e, $08, $02, $02, $03, $04, $05, $0c .byte $02, $02, $01, $01, $02, $02, $03, $03 ; level 26 .byte $04, $05, $0c, $0d, $0f, $0f, $0f, $0c .byte $02, $02, $10, $02, $0b, $02, $10, $02 .byte $0b, $02, $07, $0e, $0e, $08, $07, $08 .byte $02, $02, $02, $07, $0e, $08, $07, $08 ; level 27 .byte $07, $0e, $08, $07, $0e, $0e, $08, $02 .byte $02, $02, $03, $04, $05, $0c, $0b, $02 .byte $02, $02, $03, $03, $01, $01, $02, $02 .byte $02, $0d, $0c, $04, $05, $02, $02, $02 ; level 28 .byte $0d, $0c, $04, $05, $02, $02, $02, $07 .byte $08, $07, $08, $02, $02, $03, $03, $02 .byte $0d, $0c, $04, $05, $02, $02, $07, $08 .byte $02, $02, $02, $07, $08, $06, $07, $0e ; level 29 .byte $08, $06, $07, $08, $03, $03, $02, $02 .byte $0b, $02, $10, $07, $08, $07, $0e, $08 .byte $02, $02, $0b, $02, $10, $02, $10, $02 .byte $01, $02, $03, $04, $05, $0c, $0d, $0c ; level 30 .byte $02, $0b, $02, $10, $02, $10, $07, $08 .byte $07, $0e, $08, $07, $0e, $08, $02, $02 .byte $01, $01, $02, $02, $03, $04, $05, $02 .byte $01, $02, $02, $03, $03, $04, $09, $05 ; level 31 .byte $02, $0d, $0f, $0c, $0a, $0c, $02, $06 .byte $06, $07, $08, $07, $0e, $08, $02, $0b .byte $0d, $0f, $0c, $10, $02, $03, $04, $05 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Applies a buffered vertical offset to Sprite 0 (the player's car). ; Takes the accumulated vertical offset stored at $0808, adds it to the current ; Y position of Sprite 0 ($D001), and then clears $0808 back to 0. This is ; used to process collision bumps, road bumps, or other vertical shifts pending ; for the frame. ; ; Inputs: Y position at $D001, offset at $0808 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies Sprite 0 Y pos ($D001) and sets $0808 to 0. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- apply_y_offset lda $d001 ; x-ref: $182a, $4768; Sprite 0 Y Pos clc adc a0808 ; Add offset buffered in $0808 sta $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos lda #$00 ; Clear the buffered offset sta a0808 rts ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Handles player acceleration (Up direction) and deceleration (Down direction). ; Reads the parsed input state at $00F5. If 'Up' (Bit 0) is pressed, the car ; moves up the screen (Y position at $D001 decreases until clamped at $90), ; the scrolling speed $0804 is increased, and the 3-byte visual speedometer array ; at $08E0 is incremented in base-10. ; If 'Down' is pressed, it branches to $4464 which performs the inverse (speed ; array decrements, car moves down the screen until clamped at $D0). ; ; Inputs: Parsed input state at $00F5 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies Sprite 0 Y pos ($D001), speed $0804, and speedometer $08E0 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- handle_acceleration lda @w joy_state ; x-ref: $17d4, $4770 and #$0f ; Check Bit 0 (Up/Forward) and #$01 bne b4460 ; Branch if not pressed to check Down lda $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos cmp #$90 beq b4429 lda $d001 ; Move Car UP 1 pixel; Sprite 0 Y Pos sec sbc #$01 sta $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos b4429 inc player_speed ; Increase scroll speed ($0804) x2 ; x-ref: $441e inc player_speed ldy #$00 b4431 ldx #$02 ; x-ref: $444c j4433 lda a08E0,x ; Base-10 Increment Speedometer array ($08E0) ; x-ref: $4446 clc adc #$01 sta a08E0,x cmp #$0a bne b4449 lda #$00 sta a08E0,x dex jmp j4433 b4449 iny ; x-ref: $443e cpy #$02 bne b4431 lda player_speed cmp #$de bne b445F lda #$dc sta player_speed lda #$00 sta a08E2 b445F rts ; x-ref: $4453 b4460 jmp j49ED ; x-ref: $4417 nop handle_deceleration lda $d001 ; x-ref: $49f4; Sprite 0 Y Pos cmp #$d0 beq b4474 lda $d001 ; Move Car DOWN 1 pixel; Sprite 0 Y Pos clc adc #$01 sta $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos b4474 dec player_speed ; x-ref: $4469 dec player_speed ldy #$00 b447C ldx #$02 ; x-ref: $4497 j447E lda a08E0,x ; Base-10 Decrement Speedometer array ($08E0) ; x-ref: $4491 sec sbc #$01 sta a08E0,x cmp #$ff bne b4494 lda #$09 sta a08E0,x dex jmp j447E b4494 iny ; x-ref: $4489 cpy #$02 bne b447C lda player_speed cmp #$12 bne b44AF lda #$14 sta player_speed lda #$00 sta a08E2 lda #$02 sta a08E1 b44AF rts ; x-ref: $449e ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Initializes the game's HUD/Dashboard at the bottom of the screen. ; Draws the border graphics and prints the numeric values from $08E0. ; ; Inputs: $08E0..$08E2 (3-byte numeric value) ; Outputs: Screen RAM ($0772-$07CA), Color RAM ($DB72-$DBCA) ; Side Effects: Updates rows 22-24 of the screen display. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- init_hud ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $11f0 b44B2 lda a08E0,x ; Loop through 3 digits at $08E0 ; x-ref: $44c3 clc adc #$30 ; Convert to PETSCII '0'-'9' sta f079B,x ; Store in Row 23, Column 3-5 lda #$03 sta fDB9B,x inx cpx #$03 bne b44B2 nop lda #$24 sta a0772 ldx #$00 b44CD lda #$25 ; Loop to draw 7 border chars ; x-ref: $44da sta f0773,x lda #$29 sta f07C3,x inx cpx #$07 bne b44CD lda #$26 sta a077A lda #$27 sta a07A2 lda #$28 sta a07CA lda #$2a sta a07C2 lda #$0d sta a079F lda #$10 sta a07A0 lda #$08 sta a07A1 ldx #$00 lda #$07 ; yellow b4503 sta fDB72,x ; x-ref: $450f sta fDB9A,x sta fDBC2,x inx cpx #$09 bne b4503 lda #$2b jsr s451D rts .byte $20, $b0, $44, $4c, $46, $63 s451D sta a079A ; x-ref: $4513 lda #$20 sta a079E rts ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Updates the game state by decrementing the progress counter and checking for ; collisions between the player car (Sprite 0) and enemy cars (Sprites 1-4). ; ; 1. Decrements the 3-byte BCD counter at $08E0 by 20 units. ; 2. Checks for 16x16 pixel overlaps between Sprite 0 and Sprites 1-4. ; 3. If collision detected, sets car_state to 2 and calls handler s3EEE. ; ; Inputs: $08E0 (HUD counter), car_state (car status), VIC sprite registers ; Outputs: $08E0 (decremented), car_state (updated on collision) ; Side Effects: Calls collision handler s3EEE on impact. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- update_tick_and_collisions txa ; x-ref: $4c38 pha tya pha ldx #$00 lda player_speed sec sbc #$14 sta player_speed b4535 ldy #$02 ; x-ref: $4550 j4537 lda a08E0,y ; Decrement BCD counter 20 times ; x-ref: $454a sec sbc #$01 sta a08E0,y cmp #$ff bne b454D lda #$09 sta a08E0,y dey jmp j4537 b454D inx ; x-ref: $4542 cpx #$14 bne b4535 ldx #$00 ; Check if enemy car is active b4554 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x ; x-ref: $455e cmp #$01 beq b4565 j455B inx ; x-ref: $457e, $45a4, $45ed cpx #$04 bne b4554 pla tay pla tax rts b4565 txa ; x-ref: $4559 pha asl a tax lda $d002,x ; X-coord collision check (16px); Sprite 1 X Pos cmp $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos bcc b4581 lda $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos sec sbc $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos cmp #$10 bcc b458F j457C pla ; x-ref: $458c tax jmp j455B b4581 lda $d000 ; x-ref: $456f; Sprite 0 X Pos sec sbc $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos cmp #$10 bcc b458F jmp j457C b458F lda $d003,x ; x-ref: $457a, $458a; Sprite 1 Y Pos cmp $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos bcc b45A7 lda $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos sec sbc $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos cmp #$10 bcc b45B5 j45A2 pla ; x-ref: $45b2 tax jmp j455B b45A7 lda $d001 ; x-ref: $4595; Sprite 0 Y Pos sec sbc $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos cmp #$10 bcc b45B5 jmp j45A2 b45B5 pla ; x-ref: $45a0, $45b0 tax lda #$02 ; Collision! Set color/state to 2 jsr s3EEE lda #$01 sta $d028,x ; Sprite 1 Color lda tbl_enemy_car_type,x tay lda tbl_points_sprite_frame,y jsr award_collision_score_and_damage_sprite lda #$10 sta f0930,x ldy #$00 b45D2 lda a0980,y ; x-ref: $45da beq b45DE iny cpy #$03 bne b45D2 ldy #$00 b45DE lda #$e2 ; x-ref: $45d5 sta a07FD,y lda #$01 sta a0980,y lda #$10 jsr s4E3D jmp j455B s45F0 lda #$07 ; x-ref: $13b4 sta $d015 ; Sprite display Enable lda #$ff sta $d01c ; Sprites Multi-Color Mode Select ldx season_nr rts .byte $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Dynamic delay loop to govern the frame rate and scrolling speed. ; Subtracts the current speed value ($0804) from a base constant ($F3). ; The resulting value determines how many times a nested busy-wait loop executes. ; The faster the car is going (higher $0804 value), the smaller the delay count, ; resulting in the frame executing more quickly. ; ; Inputs: Speed variable at $0804 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Busy waits (delays CPU execution) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- delay_frame_by_speed .block lda #$f3 ; Base delay count ; x-ref: $1835, $4773 sec sbc player_speed ; Subtract current speed tax l00 ldy #$30 ; Outer loop count (X register) ; x-ref: $460d l01 dey ; Inner busy loop (Y register) ; x-ref: $460a bne l01 dex bne l00 rts .bend ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Displays the "GAME OVER" message centered on row 9 and pauses execution for ; approximately 2.6 seconds. ; ; Inputs: None ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Writes to Screen RAM ($0578) and Color RAM ($D978) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- show_game_over_and_wait .block ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $1b5d l00 lda txt_game_over,x ; Read 'GAME OVER' character from $1FF7 ; x-ref: $4620 sta f0578,x ; Write char to centered screen row 9 lda #$03 ; color cyan sta fD978,x inx cpx #$09 bne l00 lda #$08 ; Approx 8 * 256 * 256 iterations (~2.6s) sta a08C0 l01 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $4634 l02 ldy #$00 ; x-ref: $462f l03 dey ; x-ref: $462c bne l03 dex bne l02 dec a08C0 bne l01 rts .bend ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Handles screen interactions by calculating Color RAM pointers and checking ; for specific coordinate hotspots (Row 9, Col $1B/$21) to trigger screen cleanup. ; ; Inputs: active_row ($0860), active_col ($0861), state_cleanup_index ($0857) ; Outputs: p_color_ram ($49/$4A), reset flags at $0858/$0859 ; Side Effects: Modifies Screen RAM and internal buffers (erases elements) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- handle_screen_hotspots lda #>fD800 ; x-ref: $3fc1 sta p_tmp_hi lda active_col sta p_tmp_lo ldy active_row j4643 beq b4656 ; x-ref: $4653 lda p_tmp_lo clc adc #$28 sta p_tmp_lo lda p_tmp_hi adc #$00 sta p_tmp_hi dey jmp j4643 b4656 jmp j1EDD ; Jumps to a location that jumps to the next instruction ; x-ref: $4643 j4659 lda active_row ; x-ref: $1edd cmp #$09 bne b4667 lda active_col cmp #$1b beq b4678 b4667 lda active_row ; x-ref: $465e cmp #$09 bne b4675 lda active_col cmp #$21 beq terminate_name_entry b4675 jmp handle_name_letter_selection ; x-ref: $466c b4678 lda name_entry_substate ; x-ref: $4665 cmp #$01 bne b468B jsr name_entry_backspace sta f7430,x sta f0618,x jmp decrement_timer b468B cmp #$02 ; x-ref: $467d bne b469B jsr name_entry_backspace sta f7438,x sta f0668,x jmp decrement_timer b469B cmp #$03 ; x-ref: $468d bne b46AB jsr name_entry_backspace sta f7440,x sta f06B8,x jmp decrement_timer b46AB cmp #$04 ; x-ref: $469d bne b46BB jsr name_entry_backspace sta f7448,x sta f0708,x jmp decrement_timer b46BB jsr name_entry_backspace ; x-ref: $46ad sta f7450,x sta f0758,x jmp decrement_timer terminate_name_entry lda #$00 ; x-ref: $1ef9, $4673 sta highscore_timer_tens lda #$00 sta highscore_timer_ones jmp b1DC8 name_entry_backspace dex ; x-ref: $467f, $468f, $469f, $46af, $46bb cpx #$ff bne b46DB ldx #$00 b46DB lda #$20 ; x-ref: $46d7 rts check_tile_is_obstacle cmp #$20 ; x-ref: $1b38, $1b3f, $1b4a, $1b51 bne b46E3 b46E2 rts ; x-ref: $46ef b46E3 cmp #$65 ; x-ref: $46e0 beq b4728 pla pla jmp j1B55 sync_player_overlay_sprite lda pickup_mode_active ; x-ref: $4748 bne b46E2 lda #$dd sta a07FF lda $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos sta $d00e ; Sprite 7 X Pos lda $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos sta $d00f ; Sprite 7 Y Pos lda #$01 sta $d02e ; Sprite 7 Color lda #$01 sta pickup_mode_active lda #$20 sta pickup_display_timer rts ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Updates the temporary display timer for a pickup/coin sprite. ; When the player hits a specific map tile (e.g., $65), Sprite 7 is spawned as ; a visual indicator of the points gained, and state flag $085E is set. ; This routine runs each frame to tick down its lifespan timer at $085F. ; Once the timer hits 0, it deactivates the flag and hides Sprite 7 off-screen. ; ; Inputs: Pickup Active Flag at $085E, Timer at $085F ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies $085E, $085F, and Sprite 7 X Pos ($D00E) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- update_pickup_timer lda pickup_mode_active ; Check if pickup sprite is active ; x-ref: $1838, $4776 bne b4718 b4717 rts ; x-ref: $471b b4718 dec pickup_display_timer ; Decrement duration timer ; x-ref: $4715 bne b4717 lda #$00 ; Turn off active flag sta pickup_mode_active lda #$00 ; Hide Sprite 7 off-screen sta $d00e ; Sprite 7 X Pos rts b4728 lda pickup_mode_active ; x-ref: $46e5 bne b4748 ldx #$03 j472F lda score_7digits,x ; x-ref: $4742 clc adc #$01 sta score_7digits,x cmp #$0a bne b4745 lda #$00 sta score_7digits,x dex jmp j472F b4745 jsr update_hud_display ; x-ref: $473a b4748 jmp sync_player_overlay_sprite ; x-ref: $472b ; -=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-= is_speed_above_100_else_return lda player_speed ; x-ref: $1a0d cmp #100 bmi b4753 rts b4753 pla ; x-ref: $4750 pla rts ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Main gameplay tick routine. ; This is the core subroutine called every frame/tick during gameplay to process ; all major game systems. It updates the HUD, increments the score, handles player ; input and movement, processes jumps and background collisions, applies vertical ; offsets, handles acceleration (if enabled), manages frame delays, and ticks down ; the pickup/coin visual indicator. ; ; Inputs: None (relies on global state) ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Updates player coordinates, animations, score, HUD, and timers. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- tick_gameplay_systems jsr update_hud_display ; x-ref: $13aa jsr increment_score jsr handle_player_movement jsr check_jump_input jsr update_jump_animation jsr check_background_collision jsr apply_y_offset lda player_is_jumping bne b4773 jsr handle_acceleration b4773 jsr delay_frame_by_speed ; x-ref: $476e jsr update_pickup_timer jsr update_enemy_cars jsr update_explosion_animations rts .byte $00, $00, $60, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; award_collision_score_and_damage_sprite ; ; Called when the player's car lands on (or collides with) an NPC car. ; On entry, X = NPC slot index (0-3), Y = car type index from f092C,x. ; ; Phase 1 — Score award: ; Looks up the NPC's sprite frame byte from f3F18,y: ; $E3 (small car) => add 5 points to score array f0825 ; $E4 (medium car) => add 3 points ; $E5 (large car) => add 2 points ; Score is stored as individual BCD-style digits at f0825 (7 bytes, ; index 4=ones ... index 0=highest). The loop ripple-carries from ; digit 4 upward for as many iterations as the point value (Y). ; Then calls update_hud_display to redraw the score on the HUD. ; ; Phase 2 — Sprite damage: ; After restoring X/Y, re-reads f3F18,y (same frame byte) and ; falls through to j4E03, which: ; - Stores the frame byte into a07F9,x (sprite pointer for NPC slot) ; - Builds a single-bit mask for slot X, inverts it, ANDs it into ; $D01C (Sprites Multi-Color Select), switching that NPC sprite ; from multicolor to hires mode (visual 'hit' state) ; - Increments a0821 (hit sub-counter); when it reaches 10, ; resets to 0 and increments hud_level_digit ; ; Inputs: X = NPC car slot index (0-3) ; Y = car type index (f092C,x), used to index f3F18 ; Outputs: None (returns via j4E03/RTS at $4E3C) ; Side Effects: f0825 score digits updated; $D01C multicolor bit cleared ; for the hit NPC sprite; a0821 and hud_level_digit may ; increment; HUD redrawn via update_hud_display ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- award_collision_score_and_damage_sprite txa ; Save X (NPC slot) and Y (car type index) — restored at $47D9 ; x-ref: $3c8f, $45c8 pha tya pha lda tbl_points_sprite_frame,y cmp #$e3 ; $E3 = 500 pts bne b47B0 ldy #$05 ; 05 == 500 pts jmp j47BB b47B0 cmp #$e4 ; $E4 = 300 pts ; x-ref: $47a9 bne b47B9 ldy #$03 ; 03 = 300 pts jmp j47BB b47B9 ldy #$02 ; Assume frame pointer is $E5, which is 200 pts ; x-ref: $47b2 j47BB ldx #$04 ; Start ripple-carry loop at digit index 4 (hundreds) ; x-ref: $47ad, $47b6, $47d4 j47BD lda score_7digits,x ; Load current score digit ; x-ref: $47d0 clc adc #$01 ; Increment digit by 1 sta score_7digits,x cmp #$0a ; Overflow at 10? (BCD-style carry) bne b47D3 lda #$00 ; Reset digit to 0, carry to next column sta score_7digits,x dex ; Move left to next score digit jmp j47BD b47D3 dey ; Repeat for each point unit (Y times total) ; x-ref: $47c8 bne j47BB jsr update_hud_display ; Redraw HUD score display after all digits updated pla ; Restore Y (car type index) and X (NPC slot) tay pla tax lda tbl_points_sprite_frame,y ; Re-read sprite frame byte for this car type jmp j4E03 ; Fall into j4E03: store frame, clear D01C multicolor bit for this sprite slot ldx #$00 spr0_anim_frame lda sprite_ptr_0,x ; x-ref: $47f7 and #$01 beq b47FA lda sprite_ptr_0,x and #$fe sta sprite_ptr_0,x j47F4 inx ; x-ref: $499d cpx #$05 bne spr0_anim_frame rts b47FA jmp spr0_anim_next_frame ; x-ref: $47ea .byte $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Handles the player's death sequence: decrements lives, plays an explosion ; animation based on the hazard, and jumps to either "Game Over" or scans ; backward through level map data (starting at $4000) for a valid respawn ; checkpoint. ; ; Inputs: lives_ones, lives_tens, (p_tmp_lo),Y (hazard type), level_nr, a0801 ; Outputs: lives_ones, lives_tens, a0801 (respawn offset), p_tmp_lo/hi ; Side Effects: Modifies sprite_ptr_0 ($07F8), disables sprites ($D015 = 0) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- player_death_handler dec lives_ones ; x-ref: $1b55 lda lives_ones cmp #$ff bne b481E lda #$09 sta lives_ones dec lives_tens lda lives_tens cmp #$ff bne b481E lda #$00 sta lives_tens b481E lda (p_tmp_lo),y ; x-ref: $4808, $4817 cmp #$61 bne b4844 lda #$de ; explosion sprite #0 jsr s3ECB jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$df ; explosion sprite #1 sta sprite_ptr_0 jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$de ; explosion sprite #0 sta sprite_ptr_0 jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$00 sta $d015 ; disable all sprites; Sprite display Enable jmp j4861 b4844 lda #$e0 ; explosion sprite #2 ; x-ref: $4822 jsr s51DB jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$e1 ; explosion sprite #3 sta sprite_ptr_0 jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$e0 ; explosion sprite #2 sta sprite_ptr_0 jsr busy_wait_delay lda #$00 sta $d015 ; disable all sprites; Sprite display Enable j4861 lda lives_ones ; x-ref: $4841 cmp #$00 beq b486B jmp j4873 b486B lda lives_tens ; x-ref: $4866 bne j4873 jmp j1B5D j4873 lda #24 ; x-ref: $4868, $486e sta level_tile_y_offset ldy level_nr lda #level_tile_tbl sta p_tmp_hi j4883 cpy #$00 ; x-ref: $4895 beq b4898 lda p_tmp_lo clc adc #$20 sta p_tmp_lo lda p_tmp_hi adc #$00 sta p_tmp_hi dey jmp j4883 b4898 ldy level_block_idx ; x-ref: $4885 j489B lda (p_tmp_lo),y ; x-ref: $48bc cmp #$02 b489F beq b48B5 ; x-ref: $48af, $48b3 cmp #$03 beq b48B5 cmp #$01 beq b48B5 jmp j48BB .byte $f2 ; Invalid or partial instruction cmp #$0e beq b489F cmp #$0f beq b489F b48B5 sty level_block_idx ; x-ref: $489f, $48a3, $48a7 jmp j507B j48BB dey ; x-ref: $48a9 jmp j489B ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; DEAD CODE - unreferenced draft of the scroll-row modular delta calculation. ; ; No JSR, JMP, or address-table entry points to this block anywhere in the binary. ; It computes (scroll_row_snapshot - scroll_row_counter) mod 10, the arithmetic ; complement of advance_distance_counter ($4940), which computes the identical ; delta in the opposite operand order. The CPX #$00 at $48DC has no branch and ; is never acted upon. The two padding bytes ($00 $FE) at $48DE-$48DF confirm ; this was abandoned before the entry point for busy_wait_delay was stitched in ; at $48E0. ; ; This is almost certainly an earlier draft of advance_distance_counter ($4940) ; that was left in place when the final version was written four pages later. ; ; Inputs: scroll_row_snapshot ($0986), scroll_row_counter ($0827) ; Outputs: X = (snapshot - counter) mod 10 [result never used] ; Side Effects: none (dead code, never executed) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- dead_snapshot_delta_draft lda scroll_row_snapshot ; DEAD CODE: load snapshot (note: operand order is reversed vs advance_distance_counter at $4940) cmp scroll_row_counter ; compare snapshot against current counter bmi b48D1 ; snapshot < counter -> counter has NOT wrapped; take no-wrap path lda scroll_row_snapshot ; no-wrap path: delta = snapshot - counter (0..9) sec sbc scroll_row_counter jmp j48DB b48D1 lda scroll_row_counter ; wrap path: counter wrapped through 0 since snapshot was taken ; x-ref: $48c5 sec sbc scroll_row_snapshot ; partial subtraction (negative without correction) clc adc #$0a ; +10 corrects for modular wrap: delta = (snapshot - counter) + 10 j48DB tax ; X = delta mod 10; result is never used (no branch on CPX below) ; x-ref: $48ce cpx #$00 ; CPX #$00 with no subsequent branch - dead; falls into padding bytes .byte $00, $fe ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Busy-wait delay of approximately 0.67 seconds, used to pace the crash/explosion ; animation in the game-over sequence. ; ; The routine burns CPU cycles with a three-level nested countdown loop: ; outer : $08FF counts down from 2 to 0 (2 passes) ; middle : X counts down from 0 to 0 (wraps) (256 iterations per pass) ; inner : Y counts down from 0 to 0 (wraps) (256 iterations per X step) ; ; Total inner iterations = 2 x 256 x 256 = 131,072. ; At ~5 cycles per DEY/BNE pair on PAL C64 (985 kHz) this is roughly 0.67 s. ; ; Called three times in succession during the crash sprite swap sequence at j4800 ; ($4829, $4831, $4839 / $4849, $4851, $4859), producing a ~2-second animated ; pause while the explosion sprite frames cycle and the SID crash sound plays. ; ; Note: $48F4-$48F9 are dead bytes (a BNE/DEX/BNE sequence that is unreachable ; because the DEC $08FF / BNE loop exits only when $08FF reaches 0, at which ; point the BNE at $48F2 falls through directly to RTS at $48F4). ; ; Inputs: none ; Outputs: A = 0, X = 0, Y = 1 (loop residue); $08FF = 0 ; Side Effects: ~0.67 s of CPU time consumed; $08FF zeroed ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- busy_wait_delay .block lda #$02 ; outer loop counter = 2 passes ; x-ref: $4829, $4831, $4839, $4849, $4851, ... sta a08FF ; store outer count in $08FF l00 ldx #$00 ; middle loop: X = 256 iterations per outer pass ; x-ref: $48f2 l01 ldy #$00 ; inner loop: Y = 256 iterations per X step ; x-ref: $48ed l02 dey ; inner burn: ~5 cycles per iteration ; x-ref: $48ea bne l02 dex ; middle step: 256 inner loops done, decrement X bne l01 dec a08FF ; decrement outer counter ($08FF); loop back if not zero l03 bne l00 ; x-ref: $48f8 l04 rts ; return after ~0.67 s delay (2 x 256 x 256 inner iterations) ; x-ref: $48f5 bne l04 ; DEAD: unreachable - BNE/DEX/BNE below $48F4 never executed dex bne l03 rts .bend .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Advances a 4-stage pipeline of road colour-row data used by the scrolling engine. ; ; The game maintains four pipeline slots, each 40 bytes apart in RAM: ; Slot 0 (newest): ptr $0748/$0749, colour strip $0753..$076F (29 bytes) ; Slot 1: ptr $0770/$0771, colour strip $077B..$0797 ; Slot 2: ptr $0798/$0799, colour strip $07A3..$07BF ; Slot 3 (oldest): ptr $07C0/$07C1, colour strip $07CB..$07E7 ; ; Each call shifts all slots one step toward the screen bottom (slot 3 drops off): ; strip[2] -> strip[3] (29 bytes, Y = 0..28) ; strip[1] -> strip[2] ; strip[0] -> strip[1] ; ptr[2] -> ptr[3] (16-bit colour-RAM row address) ; ptr[1] -> ptr[2] ; ptr[0] -> ptr[1] ; $0748/$0749 -> ptr[0] (freshest pointer, injected each frame by the map scroller) ; ; Slot 0 ($0753 strip + $0748 ptr) is written by the caller with the new road row ; data before this routine runs, making it the "front" of the FIFO queue. ; ; Returns A = $07 so that scroll_screen_down ($1500) can immediately form the ; screen pointer $0747 (a4A=$07, a49=$47 = last byte of the visible play area), ; avoiding a redundant LDA #$07. ; ; Called once per frame by scroll_screen_down ($1500) as the first step of the ; full downward screen scroll (road flows top-to-bottom in this top-down racer). ; ; Inputs: $0753..$076F colour strip, slot 0 (29 bytes, newest row) ; $077B..$0797 colour strip, slot 1 ; $07A3..$07BF colour strip, slot 2 ; $0748/$0749 colour-RAM row pointer, slot 0 ; $0770/$0771 colour-RAM row pointer, slot 1 ; $0798/$0799 colour-RAM row pointer, slot 2 ; Outputs: A = $07 (high byte for screen-RAM scroll pointer $0747) ; $077B..$07E7 slots 1-3 colour strips overwritten with shifted data ; $0770/$0771, $0798/$0799, $07C0/$07C1 colour-RAM ptrs shifted ; Side Effects: Slot 3 previous content destroyed; slot 0 inputs are not touched. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- shift_colour_row_pipeline .block ldy #$00 ; Y = 0, loop index for 29-byte colour strip copy ; x-ref: $1500 l00 lda f07A3,y ; read slot 2 strip byte [y] ($07A3+y) ; x-ref: $4917 sta f07CB,y ; write to slot 3 strip byte [y] ($07CB+y) -- slot2 -> slot3 lda f077B,y ; read slot 1 strip byte [y] ($077B+y) sta f07A3,y ; write to slot 2 strip byte [y] ($07A3+y) -- slot1 -> slot2 lda f0753,y ; read slot 0 strip byte [y] ($0753+y) -- newest row sta f077B,y ; write to slot 1 strip byte [y] ($077B+y) -- slot0 -> slot1 iny cpy #$1d ; 29 bytes per strip (not 40; word ptrs live in the gap above $076F) bne l00 lda a0798 ; slot 1 colour-RAM row ptr lo ($0798) ... sta a07C0 ; ... -> slot 2 ptr lo ($07C0) -- slot1 ptr -> slot2 ptr lda a0799 ; slot 1 colour-RAM row ptr hi ($0799) sta a07C1 ; -> slot 2 ptr hi ($07C1) lda a0770 ; slot 0 colour-RAM row ptr lo ($0770) sta a0798 ; -> slot 1 ptr lo ($0798) -- slot0 ptr -> slot1 ptr lda a0771 ; slot 0 colour-RAM row ptr hi ($0771) sta a0799 ; -> slot 1 ptr hi ($0799) lda a0748 ; incoming slot ptr lo ($0748) -- freshest ptr, set by caller/loader sta a0770 ; -> slot 0 ptr lo ($0770) lda a0749 ; incoming slot ptr hi ($0749) sta a0771 ; -> slot 0 ptr hi ($0771) lda #>p0747 ; return A=$07: scroll_screen_down uses this as high byte of ptr $0747 rts .bend ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Advances the two-digit on-screen distance counter shown in the HUD, driven by ; how many new road rows have scrolled since the last call. ; ; scroll_row_counter ($0827) is a 0-9 modular counter incremented once per road ; row by the map scroller. Each call computes delta = (current - snapshot) mod 10 ; to get the number of new rows that have passed. For each delta tick the ; sub-step counter dist_substep ($0987) is incremented; once it reaches 7 the ; ones digit dist_digit_ones ($0823) advances by 1, carrying into the tens digit ; dist_digit_tens ($0822) on overflow. Every digit advance also fires a short SID ; voice-1 "tick" click via sid_tick_sfx_and_get_ones. ; ; The routine does NOT end with RTS. It tail-calls update_hiscore_if_beaten ; ($17DD), which compares the 7-digit score ($0825-$082B) against the stored ; hi-score ($0F50-$0F56) and copies the score over if it is higher. That routine ; returns with A = hud_level_digit ($0820), which propagates back to the caller ; update_hud_display ($16B0) and is written to screen RAM at $0420 as the level ; digit after adding $30. ; ; Inputs: scroll_row_counter ($0827) current 0-9 row counter ; scroll_row_snapshot ($0986) row counter value at previous call ; dist_substep ($0987) sub-step accumulator (0-6) ; dist_digit_ones ($0823) ones digit of distance counter (0-9) ; dist_digit_tens ($0822) tens digit of distance counter (0-9) ; Outputs: A = hud_level_digit ($0820) (returned via tail-call to $17DD) ; dist_digit_ones and dist_digit_tens updated in place ; Side Effects: scroll_row_snapshot ($0986) updated to current scroll_row_counter ; dist_substep ($0987) updated ; SID voice 1 programmed with tick SFX on each digit advance ; Hi-score at $0F50-$0F56 overwritten if current score is higher ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- advance_distance_counter lda scroll_row_counter ; load current scroll-row counter (0-9, wraps each 10 rows) ; x-ref: $16b0 cmp scroll_row_snapshot ; compare with snapshot from previous call bmi b4952 ; current < snapshot means counter wrapped through 0; take wrap path ; no-wrap path: delta = current - snapshot (0..9) lda scroll_row_counter sec sbc scroll_row_snapshot ; delta = current - snapshot jmp j495C ; wrap path: counter passed through 0; delta = (current - snapshot) + 10 b4952 lda scroll_row_counter ; x-ref: $4946 sec sbc scroll_row_snapshot ; partial subtraction (will be negative without correction) clc adc #$0a ; +10 corrects for modular wrap: delta = (current - snapshot) + 10 j495C tax ; X = number of scroll-row ticks to consume this frame ; x-ref: $494f j495D cpx #$00 ; loop: all ticks consumed? ; x-ref: $4989 beq b498C ; yes -> save snapshot and exit lda dist_substep ; load sub-step counter (0-6); digit advances once every 7 ticks clc adc #$01 sta dist_substep cmp #$07 ; completed a full 7-step cycle? bne b4988 ; no -> skip digit advance this tick lda #$00 sta dist_substep ; reset sub-step to 0 for next cycle jsr sid_tick_sfx_and_get_ones ; fire SID voice-1 tick SFX; returns current dist_digit_ones in A clc adc #$01 ; increment ones digit sta lives_ones cmp #$0a ; ones digit reached 10? (overflow) bne b4988 ; no overflow -> continue loop lda #$00 sta lives_ones ; wrap ones digit back to 0 inc lives_tens ; carry into tens digit b4988 dex ; consume one tick; loop back ; x-ref: $496c, $497e jmp j495D b498C lda scroll_row_counter ; reload current row counter (snapshot must be fresh, not stale in A) ; x-ref: $495f sta scroll_row_snapshot ; save as new snapshot for next call jmp j1799 ; tail-call: update hi-score if beaten; returns A = hud_level_digit ($0820) spr0_anim_next_frame lda sprite_ptr_0,x ; x-ref: $47fa ora #$01 sta sprite_ptr_0,x jmp j47F4 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Updates 2-frame animations for player and active enemy car sprites. ; Toggles bit 0 of sprite pointers ($07F8-$07FC) to cycle frames. ; ; Inputs: sfx_voice_1_en ($0805) - Player animation enable flag ; car_state ($0928) - Array of 4 enemy car states (1 = Active) ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies sprite pointers in screen memory. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- update_sprite_animations lda player_is_jumping ; x-ref: $1844 beq b49AA ldx #$01 jmp j49C4 b49AA lda sprite_ptr_0 ; x-ref: $49a3 and #$01 bne b49BC ; If bit 0 set, clear it. Else set it. lda sprite_ptr_0 ora #$01 sta sprite_ptr_0 jmp j49C4 b49BC lda sprite_ptr_0 ; x-ref: $49af and #$fe sta sprite_ptr_0 j49C4 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $49a7, $49b9 b49C6 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x ; x-ref: $49ea cmp #$01 bne b49E7 lda sprite_ptr_1,x and #$01 bne b49DF lda sprite_ptr_1,x ; Toggle frame for active enemy car ora #$01 sta sprite_ptr_1,x jmp b49E7 b49DF lda sprite_ptr_1,x ; x-ref: $49d2 and #$fe sta sprite_ptr_1,x b49E7 inx ; x-ref: $49cb, $49dc cpx #$04 bne b49C6 rts ; -=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-= j49ED lda @w joy_state ; x-ref: $4460 and #$02 bne b49F7 jmp handle_deceleration b49F7 rts ; x-ref: $49f2 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Iterates through the 4 available enemy car slots ($0928). ; If it finds an empty slot, it uses the KERNAL ROM's random number generator ; rutines ($E097) multiple times to determine the enemy car's sprite shape (0-7), ; initial starting X coordinate (bounded so it stays on the road), and speed. ; ; Inputs: Enemy active array at $0928 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Spawns new enemies, modifies Sprite Pointers $07F9-$07FC, ; and relies on KERNAL ROM state. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- spawn_enemy_cars ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $3ad4 b4A02 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x ; x-ref: $4a0a beq b4A0D ; Check if slot is empty (0) j4A07 inx ; x-ref: $4abc, $4b1f cpx #$04 bne b4A02 rts b4A0D txa ; x-ref: $4a05 pha jsr ROM_RND ; Generate Random Number (uses BASIC ROM) pla tax lda a8C and #$07 ; Random 0-7 (enemy car type) sta tbl_enemy_car_type,x asl a clc adc #$c2 sta sprite_ptr_1,x txa pha jsr ROM_RND ; Generate Random Number for Position pla tax lda tbl_enemy_car_type,x cmp #$00 beq b4A4D cmp #$01 beq b4A4D cmp #$02 beq b4A5A cmp #$03 beq b4A67 cmp #$04 beq b4A74 cmp #$05 beq b4A81 cmp #$06 beq b4A81 cmp #$07 beq b4A81 brk b4A4D lda a8F ; Bounds math for enemy type 0 and 1 ; x-ref: $4a2e, $4a32 and #$7f clc adc #$67 jsr spawn_npc_car jmp j4A8B b4A5A lda a8F ; x-ref: $4a36 and #$3f clc adc #$45 jsr spawn_npc_car jmp j4A8B b4A67 lda a8F ; x-ref: $4a3a and #$7f clc adc #$7b jsr spawn_npc_car jmp j4A8B b4A74 lda a8F ; x-ref: $4a3e and #$3f clc adc #$a0 jsr spawn_npc_car jmp j4A8B b4A81 lda a8F ; x-ref: $4a42, $4a46, $4a4a and #$7f clc adc #$65 jsr spawn_npc_car j4A8B txa ; x-ref: $4a57, $4a64, $4a71, $4a7e pha jsr ROM_RND ; Generate Random Number for Speed pla tax jmp j4C74 cmp player_speed bmi b4AA5 j4A9A lda #$07 ; x-ref: $4c7d sta p_tmp_hi lda #$73 sta p_tmp_lo jmp j4AAD b4AA5 lda #>p0450 ; x-ref: $4a98, $4c80 sta p_tmp_hi lda #= $F0), ; the routine automatically despawns it by clearing its status flag and hiding the ; sprite. ; ; Inputs: Global scroll/speed ($0804), arrays at $0900/0904/0908/090C/0924/0928 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies Sprite 1-4 X/Y coordinates ($D002-$D009) and entity states. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- update_enemy_cars ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $3af0, $4779 b4C02 lda tbl_enemy_sprite_state,x ; x-ref: $4c0a beq b4C0D b4C07 inx ; x-ref: $4c10, $4c17, $4cfd, $4d06, $4d30 cpx #$04 bne b4C02 rts b4C0D lda tbl_enemy_sprite_active,x ; x-ref: $4c05 bne b4C07 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x cmp #$01 bne b4C07 lda f0908,x tay j4C1D cpy #$00 ; x-ref: $4c58 beq b4C65 lda f0904,x beq b4C45 txa pha asl a tax inc $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos jmp j4C40 s4C30 sta sprite_ptr_0 ; x-ref: $1ade lda #$00 sta $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register jmp update_tick_and_collisions .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 j4C40 pla ; x-ref: $4c2d, $4c4c tax jmp j4C5E b4C45 txa ; x-ref: $4c24 pha asl a tax dec $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos jmp j4C40 j4C4F jsr s4DA8 ; x-ref: $4ca9 jmp j4C68 j4C55 nop ; x-ref: $4c62 nop nop jmp j4C1D .byte $00, $00, $00 j4C5E jsr check_car_on_road ; x-ref: $4c42 dey jmp j4C55 b4C65 jmp j4C9A ; x-ref: $4c1f j4C68 lda f0900,x ; x-ref: $4c52 sec sbc player_speed bcc b4CC7 jmp j4CB1 j4C74 lda f0900,x ; x-ref: $4a92 sec sbc player_speed bcc b4C80 jmp j4A9A b4C80 jmp b4AA5 ; x-ref: $4c7b j4C83 lda f0900,x ; x-ref: $4ad1 sec sbc player_speed bcc b4C8F jmp j4AD9 b4C8F jmp b4ADE ; x-ref: $4c8a sec sbc player_speed bcc b4C9B .byte $4c, $d9 j4C9A txa ; x-ref: $4c65, $4da5 b4C9B pha ; x-ref: $4c96 asl a tax lda $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos clc adc #$08 sta $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos pla tax jmp j4C4F cmp player_speed bmi b4CC7 j4CB1 lda f0900,x ; x-ref: $4c71 sec sbc player_speed lsr a lsr a lsr a clc adc #$08 cmp #$10 bmi b4CC4 lda #$10 b4CC4 jmp j4CD1 ; x-ref: $4cc0 b4CC7 lda player_speed ; x-ref: $4c6f, $4caf sec sbc f0900,x jmp j4D09 j4CD1 tay ; x-ref: $4cc4, $4d19 j4CD2 cpy #$00 ; x-ref: $4ce3 beq b4CE6 jsr s3AE0 txa pha asl a tax dec $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos pla tax dey jmp j4CD2 b4CE6 txa ; x-ref: $4cd4, $4df0 pha asl a tax jmp j4D1C .byte $03, $d0, $00, $0f b4CF1 lda #$00 ; x-ref: $4d02, $4d27 sta $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos pla tax lda #$00 sta tbl_enemy_car_state,x jmp b4C07 cmp #$f0 bpl b4CF1 pla tax jmp b4C07 j4D09 lsr a ; x-ref: $4cce lsr a lsr a cmp #$09 bmi b4D12 lda #$08 b4D12 sta p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $4d0e lda #$08 sec sbc p_tmp_lo jmp j4CD1 j4D1C lda $d003,x ; x-ref: $4cea; Sprite 1 Y Pos cmp #$80 bmi b4D2A cmp #$f0 bmi b4D2E b4D27 jmp b4CF1 ; x-ref: $4d2c b4D2A cmp #$3a ; x-ref: $4d21 bmi b4D27 b4D2E pla ; x-ref: $4d25 tax jmp b4C07 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Checks whether an enemy car (sprite N, passed in X) is on a drivable road tile. ; ; The routine converts the sprite's screen-space pixel position (from VIC-II ; $D002/$D003 registers) into a tile column (Y) and tile row (X) by subtracting ; a fixed border offset and dividing by 8 (three LSRs). It then computes a ; pointer into screen RAM at $0400 using a 16-bit base address in (a49/a4A) ; and multiplies the row by 40 ($28) to find the correct row offset. ; ; A lookup table at $F0904, indexed by sprite number X, provides a half-tile ; column adjustment (+1) to handle wide sprites that straddle two tiles. ; ; The road tile check reads the screen code at the computed (row, col) position: ; - $20 (space) = road tile -> jump to j4ED3 (car is off-road / blocked) ; - $65 (letter) = another road variant -> also treated as blocked ; ; If the car is on road (non-road tile), the routine stores $01 at f0904,x (car ; is on-road flag = active), restores registers, samples bits 0-1 of ZP $8E for ; a 2-bit value and stores it at f0908,x (road direction/surface type), then ; jumps to j4C9A (continue normal car movement logic). ; ; If the car is off-road, f0904,x is cleared ($00), and the routine redirects to ; j4ED3 which will handle the out-of-bounds / collision branch. ; ; Inputs: X = sprite/car index (0-3) ; Outputs: f0904,x = 1 if on-road, 0 if off-road; f0908,x = road surface bits ; Side Effects: Reads VIC-II sprite X/Y registers ($D002/$D003); reads screen RAM ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- check_car_on_road txa ; sprite index -> X reg (doubled for VIC pair offset) ; x-ref: $4c5e pha tya pha txa pha asl a tax lda $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos sec sbc #$10 lsr a lsr a lsr a ; subtract left border ($10 px) then >> 3 = tile column -> Y tay lda $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos sec sbc #$32 lsr a lsr a lsr a tax lda #f0400 ; base screen RAM low byte = $00 (addr $0400) sta p_tmp_hi ; base screen RAM high byte = $04 (addr $0400) j4D57 cpx #$00 ; x-ref: $4d69 beq b4D6C lda p_tmp_lo clc adc #$28 sta p_tmp_lo lda p_tmp_hi adc #$00 sta p_tmp_hi dex jmp j4D57 b4D6C pla ; $20 (space screencode) = road tile -> car is off-road/blocked ; x-ref: $4d59 tax lda f0904,x beq b4D74 iny b4D74 lda (p_tmp_lo),y ; x-ref: $4d71 cmp #$20 bne b4D7F b4D7A jmp j4ED3 ; x-ref: $4d81 nop rts b4D7F cmp #$65 ; $65 = alternate road screencode -> also off-road ; x-ref: $4d78 beq b4D7A j4D83 lda f0904,x ; not on road: clear on-road flag for this car ; x-ref: $4ee9 beq b4D90 lda #$00 sta f0904,x jmp j4D95 b4D90 lda #$01 ; x-ref: $4d86 sta f0904,x j4D95 nop ; x-ref: $4d8d nop nop pla tay pla tax pla pla lda a8E and #$03 sta f0908,x jmp j4C9A s4DA8 txa ; x-ref: $4c4f pha tya pha txa asl a tax lda $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos sec sbc #$10 lsr a lsr a lsr a tay lda $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos sec sbc #$32 lsr a lsr a lsr a tax lda #p03D9 sta p_tmp_hi j4DCB cpx #$00 ; x-ref: $4ddd beq b4DE0 lda p_tmp_lo clc adc #$28 sta p_tmp_lo lda p_tmp_hi adc #$00 sta p_tmp_hi dex jmp j4DCB b4DE0 lda (p_tmp_lo),y ; x-ref: $4dcd cmp #$20 beq b4DF3 cmp #$65 beq b4DF3 b4DEA pla ; x-ref: $4dfc tay pla tax pla pla jmp b4CE6 b4DF3 iny ; x-ref: $4de4, $4de8 lda (p_tmp_lo),y cmp #$20 beq b4DFE cmp #$65 bne b4DEA b4DFE pla ; x-ref: $4df8 tay pla tax rts j4E03 sta sprite_ptr_1,x ; x-ref: $47e0 txa pha tax lda #$02 j4E0B cpx #$00 ; x-ref: $4e11 beq b4E14 asl a dex jmp j4E0B b4E14 sta p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $4e0d lda #$ff sec sbc p_tmp_lo sta p_tmp_lo lda $d01c ; Sprites Multi-Color Mode Select and p_tmp_lo sta $d01c ; Sprites Multi-Color Mode Select pla tax inc crashed_cars_ones lda crashed_cars_ones sta crashed_cars_ones cmp #$0a bne b4E3C lda #$00 sta crashed_cars_ones inc crashed_cars_tens b4E3C rts ; x-ref: $4e32 s4E3D sta f0983,y ; x-ref: $45ea lda #$01 sta $d02c,y ; Sprite 5 Color tya asl a tay lda $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos sta $d00a,y ; Sprite 5 X Pos lda $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos sta $d00b,y ; Sprite 5 Y Pos rts ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Updates explosion and projectile animation timers. ; First, it checks the enemy pool (Sprites 1-4). If an enemy is in state $02 ; (exploding), it ticks down a duration timer at $0930. When the timer reaches 0, ; it resets their state, restores their default multi-color bit config, and hides them. ; Then, it runs a second loop over a pool of 3 secondary objects (Sprites 5-7), ; ticking their timers and dropping them downward visually by adding to their Y Pos. ; ; Inputs: Enemy states at $0928, Timers at $0930 and $0983 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies Sprites 1-7 coordinates and multi-color registers. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- update_explosion_animations ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $1841, $477c b4E57 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x ; x-ref: $4e61 cmp #$02 beq b4E66 j4E5E inx ; x-ref: $4e95 cpx #$04 bne b4E57 jmp j4E98 b4E66 dec f0930,x ; x-ref: $4e5c beq b4E6E jmp j4E98 b4E6E lda #$00 ; x-ref: $4e69 sta tbl_enemy_car_state,x txa pha lda #$02 j4E77 cpx #$00 ; x-ref: $4e7d beq b4E80 asl a dex jmp j4E77 b4E80 sta p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $4e79 lda $d01c ; Sprites Multi-Color Mode Select ora p_tmp_lo sta $d01c ; Sprites Multi-Color Mode Select pla pha asl a tax lda #$00 sta $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos pla tax jmp j4E5E j4E98 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $4e63, $4e6b b4E9A lda a0980,x ; x-ref: $4ea2 bne b4EA5 j4E9F inx ; x-ref: $4eba cpx #$03 bne b4E9A rts b4EA5 dec f0983,x ; x-ref: $4e9d bne b4EBD lda #$00 sta a0980,x txa pha asl a tax lda #$00 sta $d00a,x ; Sprite 5 X Pos j4EB8 pla ; x-ref: $4f01 tax jmp j4E9F b4EBD txa ; x-ref: $4ea8 pha asl a tax lda $d00b,x ; Sprite 5 Y Pos clc adc #$08 sta $d00b,x ; Sprite 5 Y Pos jmp j4EF5 main_loop_apply_recoil jsr apply_player_x_recoil ; x-ref: $184d jmp main_loop_finalize_and_repeat j4ED3 txa ; x-ref: $4d7a pha asl a tax lda $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos cmp #$ff beq b4EE4 pla pla tay pla tax rts b4EE4 pla ; x-ref: $4edc tax nop lda (p_tmp_lo),y jmp j4D83 spawn_npc_car sta f0900,x ; x-ref: $4a54, $4a61, $4a6e, $4a7b, $4a88 lda #$01 sta tbl_enemy_car_fresh_flag,x rts j4EF5 lda $d00b,x ; x-ref: $4eca; Sprite 5 Y Pos cmp #$20 bcs b4F01 lda #$01 sta $d00a,x ; Sprite 5 X Pos b4F01 jmp j4EB8 ; x-ref: $4efa ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Writes a 2x2 tile stamp to screen RAM when the player successfully passes ; over a type-4 NPC car (the "jump target" car). Only fires once per car ; appearance: car_fresh_flag[x] is set at spawn and cleared here after stamping. ; ; Proximity conditions (both must hold): ; Y: player sprite Y >= NPC sprite Y + $20 (player is >= 32px past the NPC) ; X: |player X - NPC X| < $40 (within 64px horizontally) ; ; Screen address: $0400 + ((npc_sprite_y - $22) >> 3) * 40 + ((npc_sprite_x - $10) >> 3) ; Tile stamp (2x2): ; $1B $1C <- top row at computed address ; $1D $1E <- bottom row 40 chars later ; ; Skipped if game state (a0800) is 0 or 3. ; ; Inputs: a0800 (game state), car_state[0..3], f092C[0..3] (car type), ; car_fresh_flag[0..3], VIC-II sprite regs $D000-$D009 ; Outputs: Screen RAM ($0400+) ; Side Effects: Clears car_fresh_flag[x] after stamping (one-shot per car) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- draw_car_overtake_mark lda level_nr ; x-ref: $4fb0 cmp #$00 ; Skip if game state 0 or 3 (not in active play) beq b4F1D cmp #$03 beq b4F1D ldx #$00 b4F11 lda tbl_enemy_car_state,x ; x-ref: $4f1b cmp #$01 ; Only check on-road/active cars (state 1) beq b4F1E b4F18 inx ; x-ref: $4f23, $4f28, $4f46, $4fac cpx #$04 bne b4F11 b4F1D rts ; x-ref: $4f09, $4f0d b4F1E lda tbl_enemy_car_type,x ; x-ref: $4f16 cmp #$04 ; Only process type-4 cars (jump target) bne b4F18 lda tbl_enemy_car_fresh_flag,x beq b4F18 ; Skip if already stamped (flag cleared after first hit) txa pha asl a tax lda $d003,x ; Check Y: is player past this car?; Sprite 1 Y Pos cmp $d001 ; Sprite 0 Y Pos bcc b4F39 jmp j4F44 b4F39 lda $d001 ; x-ref: $4f34; Sprite 0 Y Pos sec sbc $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos cmp #$20 ; Player must be >= 32px below NPC (past it in race direction) bcs b4F49 j4F44 pla ; x-ref: $4f36, $4f61 tax jmp b4F18 b4F49 lda $d002,x ; x-ref: $4f42; Sprite 1 X Pos cmp $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos bcc b4F58 sec sbc $d000 ; Sprite 0 X Pos jmp j4F5F b4F58 lda $d000 ; x-ref: $4f4f; Sprite 0 X Pos sec sbc $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos j4F5F cmp #$40 ; X proximity: must be within 64px ; x-ref: $4f55 bcs j4F44 lda $d002,x ; Sprite 1 X Pos sec sbc #$10 ; sprite_x - $10 = pixel offset from left edge; >>3 = tile column (0-39) lsr a lsr a lsr a sta p_tmp_lo lda $d003,x ; Sprite 1 Y Pos sec sbc #$22 ; sprite_y - $22 = pixel offset from top; >>3 = tile row (0-24) lsr a lsr a lsr a tay lda #$04 sta p_tmp_hi j4F7C cpy #$00 ; Multiply row by 40 (screen width) to get row byte offset ; x-ref: $4f8e beq b4F91 lda p_tmp_lo clc adc #$28 sta p_tmp_lo lda p_tmp_hi adc #$00 sta p_tmp_hi dey jmp j4F7C b4F91 lda #$1b ; Write top-left tile of 2x2 stamp ; x-ref: $4f7e sta (p_tmp_lo),y iny lda #$1c ; Write top-right tile sta (p_tmp_lo),y ldy #$28 ; Next screen row (+40 chars) lda #$1d ; Write bottom-left tile sta (p_tmp_lo),y iny lda #$1e ; Write bottom-right tile sta (p_tmp_lo),y pla tax lda #$00 ; Clear flag: don't stamp this car again sta tbl_enemy_car_fresh_flag,x jmp b4F18 brk main_loop_finalize_and_repeat jsr draw_car_overtake_mark ; x-ref: $4ed0 jsr s3AE0 jsr sync_to_keypress jmp main_loop .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Restores game variables from double buffers. ; This routine copies game state data from back buffers ($0A00/$0B00 or $0C00/$0D00) ; into the active variables pages ($0800/$0900). It chooses which buffer to pull ; from based on player/turn states in $0F00 and $0F01 (useful for 2-player modes). ; ; Inputs: $0F00, $0F01 ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Overwrites $0800-$09FF memory region. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- restore_game_vars nop ; x-ref: $170c nop nop lda number_of_players cmp #$02 beq b501C b500A ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $5021 b500C lda f0A00,x ; x-ref: $5019 sta level_nr,x lda f0B00,x sta f0900,x inx bne b500C rts b501C lda current_player_nr ; x-ref: $5008 cmp #$01 beq b500A ldx #$00 b5025 lda f0C00,x ; x-ref: $5032 sta level_nr,x lda f0D00,x sta f0900,x inx bne b5025 rts s5035 sta $d001 ; x-ref: $174d; Sprite 0 Y Pos ldx #$00 b503A lda txt_player,x ; x-ref: $5048 sta f0500,x lda #$07 sta fD900,x inx cpx #$08 bne b503A lda current_player_nr clc adc #$30 sta a0507 lda #$04 sta p_tmp_lo b5057 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $5063 b5059 ldy #$00 ; x-ref: $505f b505B dey ; x-ref: $505c bne b505B dex bne b5059 dec p_tmp_lo bne b5057 ldx #$00 b5067 lda #$20 ; x-ref: $5078 sta f0500,x ldy season_nr lda tbl_text_color_seasons,y sta fD900,x inx cpx #$08 bne b5067 rts j507B lda number_of_players ; x-ref: $48b8 cmp #$02 beq b5085 jmp j50AA b5085 jmp j5175 ; x-ref: $5080 cmp #$01 beq b50A5 j508C lda #$01 ; x-ref: $512d, $5163, $519a sta current_player_nr j5091 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $50cf b5093 lda level_nr,x ; x-ref: $50a0 sta f0C00,x lda f0900,x sta f0D00,x inx bne b5093 jmp s170C b50A5 lda #$02 ; x-ref: $508a, $513e, $5152, $51bb sta current_player_nr j50AA ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $5082, $50c5, $50cd b50AC lda level_nr,x ; x-ref: $50b9 sta f0A00,x lda f0900,x sta f0B00,x inx bne b50AC jmp s170C j50BE lda number_of_players ; x-ref: $16a9 cmp #$02 beq b50C8 jmp j50AA b50C8 lda current_player_nr ; x-ref: $50c3 cmp #$01 beq j50AA jmp j5091 s50D2 lda f8038,x ; x-ref: $1711 sta f0428,x rts ; -=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-= ; dead code lda number_of_players cmp #$02 beq b50E3 b50E0 jmp game_init_and_loop ; x-ref: $50e8 b50E3 lda current_player_nr ; x-ref: $50de cmp #$01 beq b50E0 ldx #$00 jmp j50F8 php .encode .enc "screen" txt_player .text "PLAYER " ; x-ref: $503a .endencode j50F8 .byte $ff, $49, $00, $00, $ff, $49, $33, $ff ; x-ref: $50ec ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Handles a scroll direction change event, triggered from the countdown/wait ; loop at $51E6 once it expires. It checks two state variables to decide how ; to swap the map scroll buffers, then reloads the game level via s170C. ; ; $0F00 = current scroll mode (must be $02 = "switching direction" to act) ; $0F01 = current scroll direction ($01 = scrolling up/reverse, $02 = forward) ; ; If $0F00 != $02: bail out immediately to j1803 (re-init video). ; ; If $0F00 == $02, four paths based on $0F01 + pending car data: ; ; PATH A ($0F01 != $01, pending cars at $0A22/$0A23): ; Set $0F01 = $01, copy work buffer $0800 -> $0C00, ; copy car table $0900 -> $0D00, then reload level (s170C). ; ; PATH B ($0F01 != $01, pending cars at $0A22/$0A23 absent): ; Copy work buffer $0800 -> $0A00, ; copy car table $0900 -> $0B00, then reload level (s170C). ; Set $0F01 = $02. ; ; PATH C ($0F01 == $01, pending cars at $0C22/$0C23): ; Copy work buffer $0800 -> $0A00, ; copy car table $0900 -> $0B00, then reload level (s170C). ; ; PATH D ($0F01 == $01, no pending cars): ; Set $0F01 = $01, copy work buffer $0800 -> $0C00, ; copy car table $0900 -> $0D00, then reload level (s170C). ; ; In all active paths, the 256-byte buffers are fully copied via a DEX/BNE loop. ; ; Inputs: $0F00 = scroll mode flag; $0F01 = direction; $0A22/$0A23, $0C22/$0C23 ; = pending NPC car presence flags; $0800, $0900 = source work buffers ; Outputs: $0A00/$0B00 or $0C00/$0D00 = updated scroll/car buffers; ; $0F01 = updated direction state ; Side Effects: Calls s170C (restore_game_vars + level reload), re-entering the ; main game flow from the top of the level ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- handle_scroll_direction_switch lda number_of_players ; $02 = scroll direction-change mode; anything else = bail ; x-ref: $1e22, $51fc cmp #$02 beq b510A jmp game_init_and_loop b510A lda current_player_nr ; $01 = reverse direction, $02 = forward direction ; x-ref: $5105 cmp #$01 beq b511E lda a0A23 ; check if any pending NPC cars in secondary lane buffer bne b512B lda a0A22 bne b512B jmp game_init_and_loop b511E lda a0C23 ; direction is $01: check $0C22/$0C23 pending cars instead ; x-ref: $510f bne b5150 lda a0C22 bne b5150 jmp game_init_and_loop b512B ldx #$00 ; PATH A: pending cars present in lane A; set dir=$01, swap to C/D buffers ; x-ref: $5114, $5119 b512D jmp j508C ; x-ref: $513a sta f0C00,x ; copy work screen buffer $0800 -> $0C00 (256 bytes) lda f0900,x ; copy active car table $0900 -> $0D00 (256 bytes) sta f0D00,x inx bne b512D ldx #$00 b513E jmp b50A5 ; x-ref: $514b sta level_nr,x lda f0B00,x sta f0900,x inx bne b513E jmp s170C b5150 ldx #$00 ; PATH C: direction $01, pending cars in C lane; swap to A/B buffers ; x-ref: $5121, $5126 b5152 jmp b50A5 ; x-ref: $515f sta f0A00,x ; copy work screen buffer $0800 -> $0A00 (256 bytes) lda f0900,x ; copy active car table $0900 -> $0B00 (256 bytes) sta f0B00,x inx bne b5152 ldx #$00 b5163 jmp j508C ; x-ref: $5170 sta level_nr,x lda f0D00,x sta f0900,x inx bne b5163 jmp s170C j5175 lda current_player_nr ; x-ref: $5085 cmp #$01 beq b519D lda a0A23 bne b519A lda a0A22 bne b519A ldx #$00 b5188 lda level_nr,x ; x-ref: $5195 sta f0C00,x lda f0900,x sta f0D00,x inx bne b5188 jmp s170C b519A jmp j508C ; x-ref: $517f, $5184 b519D lda a0C23 ; x-ref: $517a bne b51BB lda a0C22 bne b51BB ldx #$00 b51A9 lda level_nr,x ; x-ref: $51b6 sta f0A00,x lda f0900,x sta f0B00,x inx bne b51A9 jmp s170C b51BB jmp b50A5 ; x-ref: $51a0, $51a5 j51BE jsr reset_voice1_state ; x-ref: $51de lda #$00 sta $d406 ; Voice 1: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda #$1d sta $d405 ; Voice 1: Attack / Decay Cycle Control lda #$01 sta $d401 ; Voice 1: Frequency Control - High-Byte lda #$12 sta $d400 ; Voice 1: Frequency Control - Low-Byte lda #$81 sta $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register rts s51DB sta sprite_ptr_0 ; x-ref: $4846 jmp j51BE poll_joy_for_scroll_switch lda #$20 ; x-ref: $1e0e sta joy_poll_timeout b51E6 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $51fa b51E8 ldy #$00 ; x-ref: $51f5 b51EA lda @w joy_state ; x-ref: $51f2 and #$01 beq b51FC dey bne b51EA dex bne b51E8 dec joy_poll_timeout bne b51E6 b51FC jmp handle_scroll_direction_switch ; x-ref: $51ef .byte $ff ; Invalid or partial instruction update_jump_sfx_pitch lda player_is_jumping bne b5206 rts b5206 ldx jump_anim_frame ; x-ref: $5203 lda #$00 sta $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register sta $d405 ; Voice 1: Attack / Decay Cycle Control sta $d406 ; Voice 1: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda sid_freq_hi_tbl,x sta $d401 ; Voice 1: Frequency Control - High-Byte lda sid_freq_lo_tbl,x sta $d400 ; Voice 1: Frequency Control - Low-Byte lda #$09 sta $d405 ; Voice 1: Attack / Decay Cycle Control lda #$0f sta $d406 ; Voice 1: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda #$11 sta $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register rts set_collision_state_and_play_sfx sta tbl_enemy_sprite_state,x ; x-ref: $3a4f jsr play_crash_sfx lda #$01 b5238 rts ; x-ref: $523d ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Main-loop input synchronizer. Called every frame from j4FB0. ; ; Ties the main loop's execution pace to player key input: ; - If any key is currently held (key_scan != $7F): returns immediately, ; allowing the game loop to run at full speed. ; - If no key is held (key_scan == $7F): spins through a full ; press -> release -> press cycle before returning. ; ; The CIA keyboard scanner (j56E2) runs inside the IRQ, so key_scan is ; updated even during the spin-waits — sprite animation continues unaffected. ; ; Phase 1 ($5239): bail immediately if a key is already held ; Phase 2 ($523F): spin until any key is pressed ; Phase 3 ($5245): spin until key is released ; Phase 4 (j5270): spin until key is pressed again, then return ; ; Inputs: key_scan ($C5) ; Outputs: A = key_scan on early exit; undefined on full wait path ; Side Effects: Stalls main loop execution until a fresh keypress cycle ; completes when no key is being held ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- sync_to_keypress lda key_scan ; Phase 1: key already held? Return immediately ; x-ref: $4fb6 cmp #$7f ; $7F = no key; wait for release bne b5238 b523F lda key_scan ; Phase 2: spin until any key pressed ; x-ref: $5243 cmp #$7f ; $7F = no key; loop until a key is pressed beq b523F b5245 lda key_scan ; Phase 3: spin until key released ; x-ref: $5249 cmp #$7f ; $7F = no key; wait for release bne b5245 jmp j5270 ; Phase 4: wait for second press, then return nop nop sid_freq_hi_tbl .byte >$0893, >$099f, >$0acd, >$0b72, >$0cd8 ; x-ref: $5214 .byte >$0e6b, >$102f, >$1125, >$133f, >$083f .byte >$0025, >$8845, >$d068, >$fd8c, >$cab3 .byte >$d08c sid_freq_lo_tbl .byte <$0893, <$099f, <$0acd, <$0b72, <$0cd8 .byte <$0e6b, <$102f, <$1125, <$133f, <$083f .byte <$0025, <$8845, <$d068, <$fd8c, <$cab3 .byte <$d08c j5270 lda key_scan ; Phase 4: spin until key pressed, return (shared entry point) ; x-ref: $524b, $5274 cmp #$7f ; $7F = no key; wait for press beq j5270 ; Loop while no key rts .byte $08, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $02, $00, $ff .byte $00, $40, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 .byte $ff ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Handles user input during the main menu / selection screen. ; This routine monitors a specific key press (flagged as $F7 in the keyboard ; matrix byte $C5, likely the F1 key or spacebar) to increment a 2-digit number ; stored in $49 and $4A as ASCII/Screen Code characters. The number cycles ; from '01' up to '16', and wraps back around. It then renders those digits directly ; to Video RAM at $0651 and $0652 to provide visual feedback of the selection ; (e.g., selecting a starting level or difficulty). ; ; Inputs: $C5 (Current Key Pressed) ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Modifies $49 (units), $4A (tens), and Video RAM $0651-$0652. ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- handle_menu_selection_input lda p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $3ffa sta a0652 lda p_tmp_hi sta a0651 lda key_scan cmp #$f7 ; $F7 = %11110111 (bit 3 clear) beq b5311 rts b5311 inc level_nr_self_modifiying ; x-ref: $530e lda level_nr_self_modifiying cmp #$10 bne b5320 lda #$00 sta level_nr_self_modifiying b5320 inc p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $5319 lda p_tmp_lo cmp #$3a bne b532E lda #$30 sta p_tmp_lo inc p_tmp_hi b532E lda p_tmp_hi ; x-ref: $5326 cmp #$31 bne b5342 lda p_tmp_lo cmp #$37 bne b5342 lda #$31 sta p_tmp_lo lda #$30 sta p_tmp_hi b5342 lda p_tmp_lo ; x-ref: $5332, $5338 sta a0652 lda p_tmp_hi sta a0651 b534C lda key_scan ; x-ref: $5350 cmp #$f7 ; $F7 held — wait for release beq b534C rts .encode .enc "screen" txt_bonus_10000 .text "BONUS 10000", $ff, "@" ; x-ref: $5367 .endencode ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Displays a "BONUS 10000" message and awards bonus points. ; This routine copies the "BONUS 10000" text to the screen ($0756 area) and colors ; it purple ($04). It then calls the score increment routine twice to award the ; points, performs a nested delay loop to leave the text on-screen momentarily, ; and finally jumps back to the main flow. ; ; Inputs: None ; Outputs: None ; Side Effects: Updates Screen RAM, Color RAM, Score array, and delays execution. ; Bugs: Message says "BONUS 10000", but it actually increases it by 100000 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- award_bonus_score lda #$00 ; x-ref: $157e sta level_nr ldx #$00 b5367 lda txt_bonus_10000,x ; x-ref: $5375 sta f0756,x lda #$04 sta fDB56,x inx cpx #$0b bne b5367 jsr increment_score_by_50000 ; BUG: Message says "BONUS 10000", but it actually increments 100,000 jsr increment_score_by_50000 nop nop nop nop nop nop lda #$08 sta p_tmp_lo b5387 ldx #$00 ; x-ref: $5393 b5389 ldy #$00 ; x-ref: $538f b538B dey ; x-ref: $538c bne b538B dex bne b5389 dec p_tmp_lo bne b5387 jmp b1581 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; SFX Engine Voice 1 (Engine Sound) Handler ; ; Initializes and modulates Voice 1 on the SID to create a continuous sound ; while sfx_voice_1_en is non-zero. When zeroed, it resets the local state. ; ; Inputs: sfx_voice_1_en ($0805) - Enable flag for the Voice 1 SFX ; Outputs: sfx_voice_1_state ($7080), sfx_voice_1_freq_lo ($7081), ; sfx_voice_1_freq_mod ($7082) ; Side Effects: Updates Voice 1 registers (Frequency, Control, Attack/Decay, Sustain/Release) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- irq_handler_music lda player_is_jumping ; x-ref: $1291, $1296 beq sfx_voice_1_reset ; If sf_voice_1_en is clear, reset state ldx sfx_voice_1_state bne sfx_voice_1_modulate ; If state > 0 (already playing), branch to modulation stx sfx_voice_1_freq_lo ; Clear internal freq_lo component stx $d400 ; Clear SID Voice 1 frequency low; Voice 1: Frequency Control - Low-Byte stx $d401 ; Clear SID Voice 1 frequency high; Voice 1: Frequency Control - High-Byte stx $d405 ; Voice 1: Attack / Decay Cycle Control stx $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register inx ; Set state to 1 (initialized) stx sfx_voice_1_state inx stx sfx_voice_1_freq_mod ; Set mod factor to 2 lda #$f4 ; Sustain = $F, Release = $4 sta $d406 ; Voice 1: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda #$11 ; Gate ON + Triangle Wave sta $d404 ; Voice 1: Control Register sfx_voice_1_modulate bmi b53F0 ; If state == $80 (releasing), don't process modulation ; x-ref: $53a0 lda sfx_voice_1_freq_lo clc adc sfx_voice_1_freq_mod ; Add frequency modifier to frequency component bne sfx_voice_1_store_freq lda sfx_voice_1_freq_mod bpl sfx_voice_1_bounce lda #$10 sta $d404 ; Gate OFF (Release phase) on Voice 1; Voice 1: Control Register lda #$80 sta sfx_voice_1_state ; Set state to $80 (releasing) bne b53F0 sfx_voice_1_bounce lda #$00 ; x-ref: $53d1 sec sbc sfx_voice_1_freq_mod sta sfx_voice_1_freq_mod ; Reverse direction of modifier (negate it) lda #$fe ; Wrap-around value sfx_voice_1_store_freq sta sfx_voice_1_freq_lo ; x-ref: $53cc sta $d401 ; Update SID Voice 1 frequency high; Voice 1: Frequency Control - High-Byte b53F0 jmp j54D0 ; Chain to next IRQ routine ; x-ref: $53c3, $53dd, $53f8 sfx_voice_1_reset lda #$00 ; x-ref: $539b sta sfx_voice_1_state ; Reset Voice 1 state beq b53F0 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; IRQ-driven melody player for SID Voices 2 & 3. ; ; Called every IRQ frame. Steps through a looping note sequence in the melody ; table at $5500 using a self-modifying pointer ($5445/$5446). A rate-limiting ; timer controls the pace: 8 ticks per note, then a 3-tick gate-off gap. ; Notes alternate between Voice 2 and Voice 3 so the prior note's release ; envelope can finish on one voice while the next note starts on the other. ; ; Table encoding: ; $30 = rest (silent tick, exit immediately) ; $00-$2F = pitch index into sid_freq2_lo/hi_tbl ; >= $40 = end-of-sequence; wrap pointer back to $5500 ; ; Key $BF toggles playback on/off; pressing it immediately gates both voices. ; Waveform: triangle ($21), A=0/D=0, Sustain=$B, Release=$A. ; ; Inputs: $C5 (key scan), sfx_prev_key, sfx_v23_en, sfx_v23_timer, ; sfx_v23_idx, sfx_v23_toggle; self-mod melody ptr at $5445/$5446 ; Outputs: SID Voice 2 regs ($D407-$D40D), Voice 3 regs ($D40E-$D414) ; Side Effects: Advances self-modifying melody pointer through $5500+; ; updates sfx_prev_key, sfx_v23_en, sfx_v23_timer, ; sfx_v23_idx, sfx_v23_toggle ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- irq_handler_voice23 lda key_scan ; Load current key pressed ; x-ref: $54db, $54e3 cmp sfx_prev_key beq b541E ; If key unchanged, skip to processing sta sfx_prev_key cmp #$bf ; Check if F5 was pressed bne b541E lda #$20 sta $d40b ; Gate OFF Voice 2; Voice 2: Control Register sta $d412 ; Gate OFF Voice 3; Voice 3: Control Register lda sfx_v23_en ; Toggle the enable flag eor #$01 sta sfx_v23_en b541E lda sfx_v23_en ; x-ref: $5405, $540c bne b5430 ; If not enabled, skip processing b5423 jmp scan_keyboard_joystick ; x-ref: $5433, $5461, $5488, $54a0 sfx_prev_key .byte $40 ; x-ref: $5402, $5407 sfx_v23_en .byte $01 ; x-ref: $5416, $541b, $541e sfx_v23_timer .byte $03 ; x-ref: $5430, $5441, $54a7 sfx_v23_idx .byte $01 ; x-ref: $5435, $543c, $54a2 sfx_v23_toggle .byte $00, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00 ; x-ref: $546b, $54ac, $54b3, $54bc b5430 dec sfx_v23_timer ; Decrement wait timer ; x-ref: $5421 bne b5423 ; If timer hasn't expired, exit lda sfx_v23_idx ; Check phase: 0=play next note, 1=gate-off beq b54A2 ; idx=1: enter gate-off phase for current voice ldy #$00 sty sfx_v23_idx ; Reset to play phase (idx=0) lda #$08 sta sfx_v23_timer ; 8 IRQ ticks per note melody_lo =*+$01 melody_hi =*+$02 b5444 lda melody_tbl ; Self-modifying LDA; ptr lo=$5445, hi=$5446 walk melody table ; x-ref: $545d inc melody_lo ; Advance melody ptr low byte bne b544F inc melody_hi ; Page overflow: advance ptr high byte b544F cmp #$40 ; >= $40 = end-of-sequence sentinel ; x-ref: $544a bcc b545F lda #$55 ; Reset ptr hi to $55 (back to $5500) sta melody_hi lda #$00 sta melody_lo ; Reset ptr lo to $00 beq b5444 ; Z set by lda #$00; always branches — re-read first note b545F cmp #$30 ; $30 = rest note ; x-ref: $5451 beq b5423 ; Rest: exit without playing tax ; Load pitch index into X lda sid_freq2_hi_tbl,x ; Read frequency high byte into A tay lda sid_freq2_lo_tbl,x ; Read frequency low byte into A ldx sfx_v23_toggle ; Check voice toggle flag beq b548A ; If 0, use Voice 3. If !0, use Voice 2 ldx #$00 stx $d40b ; Voice 2: Control Register sta $d407 ; Voice 2: Frequency Control - Low-Byte sty $d408 ; Voice 2: Frequency Control - High-Byte stx $d40c ; Voice 2: Attack / Decay Cycle Control lda #$ba sta $d40d ; Voice 2: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda #$21 sta $d40b ; Voice 2: Control Register bne b5423 b548A stx $d412 ; x-ref: $546e; Voice 3: Control Register sta $d40e ; Voice 3: Frequency Control - Low-Byte sty $d40f ; Voice 3: Frequency Control - High-Byte stx $d413 ; Voice 3: Attack / Decay Cycle Control lda #$ba sta $d414 ; Voice 3: Sustain / Release Cycle Control lda #$21 sta $d412 ; Voice 3: Control Register bne b5423 b54A2 inc sfx_v23_idx ; x-ref: $5438 lda #$03 sta sfx_v23_timer ; 3-tick release gap between notes lda #$20 ; %00100000 = triangle wave, gate OFF ldx sfx_v23_toggle ; Which voice was just playing? beq b54BC ; toggle=0: V3 was playing → gate off V3 ldx #$00 stx sfx_v23_toggle ; Clear toggle → next note plays on V3 sta $d40b ; Voice 2: Control Register b54B9 jmp scan_keyboard_joystick ; x-ref: $54c2 b54BC inc sfx_v23_toggle ; Set toggle (non-zero) → next note plays on V2 ; x-ref: $54af sta $d412 ; Gate off Voice 3 (it was playing); Voice 3: Control Register bne b54B9 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00 j54D0 lda $d000 ; x-ref: $53f0; Sprite 0 X Pos cmp #$0c bcc b54DE cmp #$18 bcc b54E6 jmp irq_handler_voice23 b54DE lda #$ff ; x-ref: $54d5 b54E0 sta $d000 ; x-ref: $54e8; Sprite 0 X Pos jmp irq_handler_voice23 b54E6 lda #$18 ; x-ref: $54d9 bne b54E0 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 melody_tbl .byte $30, $12, $30, $0e, $10, $12, $30, $0e ; x-ref: $5444 .byte $12, $30, $10, $0e, $30, $09, $30, $0a .byte $0b, $0e, $10, $13, $30, $0e, $10, $0e .byte $10, $13, $30, $10, $30, $12, $0e, $10 .byte $12, $30, $0e, $12, $30, $10, $0e, $30 .byte $09, $30, $13, $30, $10, $30, $0e, $30 .byte $0b, $30, $0b, $0b, $30, $02, $04, $07 .byte $06, $30, $12, $0e, $10, $12, $30, $0e .byte $12, $30, $10, $0e, $30, $09, $30, $0a .byte $0b, $0e, $10, $13, $30, $0e, $10, $0e .byte $10, $13, $30, $10, $30, $0c, $30, $0d .byte $30, $13, $30, $10, $30, $0e, $30, $09 .byte $30, $13, $30, $30, $12, $30, $30, $13 .byte $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30 .byte $30, $30, $30, $30, $13, $10, $0e, $10 .byte $0c, $10, $0b, $10, $09, $10, $07, $10 .byte $05, $10, $0e, $0c, $0e, $0e, $0c, $0e .byte $0b, $0e, $09, $0e, $07, $0e, $05, $0e .byte $04, $0e, $0c, $0b, $0c, $0c, $0b, $0c .byte $09, $0c, $07, $0c, $05, $0c, $04, $0c .byte $03, $0c, $0b, $09, $0b, $0b, $09, $0b .byte $08, $0b, $06, $0b, $04, $0b, $02, $0b .byte $00, $0b, $09, $07, $09, $0e, $0c, $0c .byte $10, $0e, $0e, $13, $12, $13, $0e, $0b .byte $07, $09, $0b, $0c, $0e, $10, $0e, $0c .byte $0b, $09, $0b, $07, $06, $07, $09, $02 .byte $06, $09, $0c, $0b, $09, $0b, $07, $09 .byte $0b, $0e, $0c, $0c, $10, $0e, $0e, $13 .byte $12, $13, $0e, $0b, $07, $09, $0b, $04 .byte $0e, $0c, $0b, $09, $07, $02, $07, $06 .byte $07, $0b, $0e, $13, $0e, $0b, $07, $0b .byte $0e, $11, $0e, $0b, $07, $0b, $0e, $10 .byte $0c, $09, $06, $09, $0c, $0e, $0b, $07 .byte $04, $07, $0b, $0c, $09, $06, $02, $06 .byte $09, $0c, $0b, $09, $07, $0b, $0e, $13 .byte $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30, $30 .byte $80 .byte $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff .byte $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff .byte $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff, $00, $ff .byte $00, $ff, $00, $02, $00, $ff, $00 sid_freq2_lo_tbl .byte <$0893, <$0915, <$099f, <$0a3c, <$0acd ; x-ref: $5468 .byte <$0b72, <$0c20, <$0cd8, <$0d9c, <$0e6b .byte <$0f66, <$102f, <$1125, <$122a, <$133f .byte <$1464, <$159a, <$16e3, <$183f, <$19b1 .byte <$1b38, <$1cd6, <$1e8d, <$205e, <$224b .byte <$2455, <$267e, <$28c8, <$2b34, <$2dc6 .byte <$307f, <$3361, <$366f, <$39ac, <$3d7e .byte <$40bc, <$4495, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 .byte <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 .byte <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 .byte <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 .byte <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 .byte <$0000, <$0000, <$0000, <$0000 sid_freq2_hi_tbl .byte >$0893, >$0915, >$099f, >$0a3c, >$0acd .byte >$0b72, >$0c20, >$0cd8, >$0d9c, >$0e6b .byte >$0f66, >$102f, >$1125, >$122a, >$133f .byte >$1464, >$159a, >$16e3, >$183f, >$19b1 .byte >$1b38, >$1cd6, >$1e8d, >$205e, >$224b .byte >$2455, >$267e, >$28c8, >$2b34, >$2dc6 .byte >$307f, >$3361, >$366f, >$39ac, >$3d7e .byte >$40bc, >$4495, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 .byte >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 .byte >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 .byte >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 .byte >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 .byte >$0000, >$0000, >$0000, >$0000 ; Scans CIA1 keyboard matrix by driving Port B (rows) and reading Port A (cols). ; A = DDR-B mask (row to drive). Returns stable column byte in A (debounced). cia1_scan_row .block sta $dc03 ; x-ref: $56ee, $5702, $5714; CIA1: Data Direction Register B lda #$00 sta $dc02 ; CIA1: Data Direction Register A l00 lda $dc00 ; x-ref: $56ce; CIA1: Data Port Register A cmp $dc00 ; CIA1: Data Port Register A bne l00 rts .bend ; Scans CIA1 keyboard matrix by driving Port A (cols) and reading Port B (rows). ; A = DDR-A mask (column to drive). Returns stable row byte in A (debounced). cia1_scan_col .block sta $dc02 ; x-ref: $56f7, $570b, $5728; CIA1: Data Direction Register A lda #$00 sta $dc03 ; CIA1: Data Direction Register B l00 lda $dc01 ; x-ref: $56df; CIA1: Data Port Register B cmp $dc01 ; CIA1: Data Port Register B bne l00 rts .bend ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- ; Custom keyboard/joystick scanner. Replaces part of the KERNAL's standard ; keyboard scan by directly driving the CIA1 matrix to read joystick port 2 ; and specific keyboard rows/columns. Builds a combined key code and a ; joystick-format direction byte, then tail-calls into the KERNAL at $EA7E. ; ; Inputs: CIA1 hardware registers ($DC00-$DC03) ; Outputs: key_scan ($C5) = current key matrix code ($7F = no key) ; joy_state ($F5) = joystick state (active-low bits 0-4: U/D/L/R/Fire) ; Side Effects: Modifies CIA1 DDRs; tail-calls KERNAL $EA7E (no RTS) ; =-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=-=- scan_keyboard_joystick .block ldx #$04 ; Clear CIA1 ports $DC00-$DC03 (via $DBFF+X, X=4..1) ; x-ref: $5423, $54b9 lda #$00 l00 sta fDBFF,x ; x-ref: $56ea dex bne l00 lda #$10 ; Drive row 4 → read joystick fire + key row jsr cia1_scan_row ora #%01111111 ; Keep bit 7 (fire); idle bits high sta key_scan ; First scan result → key_scan lda #$01 ; Drive column 0 jsr cia1_scan_col ora #%10000111 ; Mask unused bits 3-6 and key_scan ; Combine row + column scans → final key code sta key_scan ; Final combined key state → key_scan ($7F = no key) lda #$00 ; Scan full row 0 to check for any key jsr cia1_scan_row cmp #$ff ; Key pressed → skip joystick decode bne l01 lda #$00 ; Scan full column 0 jsr cia1_scan_col cmp #$ff bne l01 ; Key pressed → skip joystick decode lda #$04 ; Drive row 2 for direction bits jsr cia1_scan_row tax ora #$ef ; Isolate bit 4 (joy right) sta joy_state txa lsr a ; Shift raw bits right ×3 for bits 2-3 lsr a lsr a ora #%11110011 ; Isolate bits 2-3 (joy up/down) and joy_state ; Merge direction bits sta joy_state lda #%00100000 ; Drive column 5 for left direction jsr cia1_scan_col lsr a ; Shift into position tax ora #%11111110 ; Isolate bit 0 (joy up) and joy_state sta joy_state txa ; Shift right ×2 more lsr a lsr a ora #%11111101 ; Isolate bit 1 (joy down) and joy_state ; Final merge of all direction bits l01 ora #%11100000 ; Set upper 3 bits high (unused, always 1) ; x-ref: $5707, $5710 sta joy_state jmp eEA7E ; Continue into KERNAL keyboard scan completion .bend .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .encode .enc "screen" .text "copyright colosoftware xrom system br" .text "ussels 1983 " .endencode .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00, $00 .byte $00, $00 .binary "c64_burnin_rubber_5800_7fff.bin" .word RESET_VECTOR, cartridge_reset_entry .byte $c3, $c2, $cd, $38, $30, $20, $20, $20 ; CBM80 .byte $20, $20, $20, $20 .encode .enc "none" ; Each "tile" consists of $0400 (1024) bytes, what fits in a screen. ; Each level consists of different "tiles" that are defined in $4000 ; block idx #00 level_tile_definition_tbl .text "EEEEEE@B BCEEEEEEE" ; x-ref: $1329, $132d, $138d, $1391 f8038 .text "EEEEEEAB BDEEEEEEE" ; x-ref: $50d2 .text "EEEEEE@B BCEEEEEEE" .text "EEEEEEAB BDEEEEEEE" .text "EEEEEE@B BCEEEEEEE" .text "EEEEEEAB BDEEEEEEE" .text "EEEEEE@B " f8110 .text " BCEEEEEEEEEEEEEAB " ;
